content:
# Magnetic Susceptibility

**Summary**: The study of magnetic properties to determine depth to geological features.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2020-abdallah-2|Abdallah, 2020]] used magnetic-susceptibility techniques (Werner and Spectral) to establish a 300m uncertainty range for the basement.

- [[2024-akomolafe|Akomolafe, 2024]] evaluated the impact of hematite's magnetic susceptibility on T2 relaxation.

## Instruments and Software



- [[2024-akomolafe|Akomolafe, 2024]] evaluated the impact of hematite's magnetic susceptibility on T2 relaxation.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- Established a reliable uncertainty range of 6300-6600 m for the depth to basement. ([[2020-abdallah-2|Abdallah, 2020, p. 72]])

- The addition of hematite... its magnetic susceptibility accelerated relaxation processes, leading to a shift toward shorter T2 times and the appearance of low T2 signals below 1 ms. ([[2024-akomolafe|Akomolafe, 2024, p. 83]])
